evidences.title: Binance API for Historical Crypto Prices | by Zachary Raicik - Medium
evidences.content: symbol: The crypto symbol of the asset (e.g. ETHUSDT) interval: The interval at which we want the request to return prices (e.g. 1d, 12h) start\_date: The day at which you want the request to start returning prices end\_date: The last day at which you want the request to start returning prices The function’s s signature will look like this: ``` def get_historical_prices( symbol: str, interval: str, start_date: dt.datetime, end_date: dt.datetime,) def get_historical_prices symbol interval start_date end_date ``` The next two bits of code take the arguments passed to the function and create the information needed by the Binance API to make a request. Note that Binance require date to be expressed as UNIX timestamps. [...] parser.add_argument( "--symbol", default="ETHUSDT", help="The symbol of the cryptocurrency to retrieve price data for (e.g. 'ETHUSDT')", ) parser.add_argument( "--interval", default="1d", help="The interval at which to retrieve price data (e.g. '1d' for daily data)", ) parser.add_argument( "--start_date", type=dt.datetime.fromisoformat, help="The start date for the price data to retrieve, in YYYY-MM-DD format", ) parser.add_argument( "--end_date", type=dt.datetim...

evidences.title: How to Download Historical Price Data from Binance with Python
evidences.content: `def get_bars(symbol, interval = '1h'): url = root_url + '?symbol=' + symbol + '&interval=' + interval data = json.loads(requests.get(url).text) df = pd.DataFrame(data) df.columns = ['open_time', 'o', 'h', 'l', 'c', 'v', 'close_time', 'qav', 'num_trades', 'taker_base_vol', 'taker_quote_vol', 'ignore'] df.index = [dt.datetime.fromtimestamp(x/1000.0) for x in df.close_time] return df` Now we can do cool stuff like download the data for STEEM/ETH and ETH/USDT: `steemeth = get_bars('STEEMETH') ethusdt = get_bars('ETHUSDT')` Now we can multiply the price of STEEM/ETH by ETH/USDT to get STEEM/USDT `steemusdt = steemeth['c'].astype('float') ethusdt['c'].astype('float')` And plot the result: `steemusdt.plot()` [...] return df steemeth = get_bars('STEEMETH') ethusdt = get_bars('ETHUSDT') steemusdt = steemeth['c'].astype('float') ethusdt['c'].astype('float') steemusdt.plot(figsize=(16,9))` [...] `import requests # for making http requests to binance import json # for parsing what binance sends back to us import pandas as pd # for storing and manipulating the data we get back import numpy as np # numerical python, i usually need this somewhere # and so i import by habit nowadays import mat...

evidences.title: Get kline/candlestick data from Binance — binance_klines
evidences.content: # Get kline/candlestick data from Binance `binance_klines.Rd` Get kline/candlestick data from Binance `binance_klines( symbol, interval = c("1m", "3m", "5m", "15m", "30m", "1h", "2h", "4h", "6h", "8h", "12h", "1d", "3d", "1w", "1M"), limit, start_time, end_time )` ## Arguments string enum optional int optional POSIX timestamp optional POSIX timestamp ## Value `data.table` with open-high-low-close values `data.table` ## Examples `if (FALSE) { binance_klines('ETHUSDT') binance_klines('ETHUSDT', interval = '1h', limit = 247) binance_klines('ETHUSDT', interval = '1h', start_time = '2018-01-01', end_time = '2018-01-08') }` ## Contents Developed by Gergely Daróczi, David Andel. evidences.title: Simple Green/Red Candle Trading Strategy for Cryptocurrency
evidences.content: Title: Simple Green/Red Candle Trading Strategy for Cryptocurrency Simple Green/Red Candle Trading Strategy for Cryptocurrency | Cryptovip on Binance Square. Simple Green/Red Candle Trading Strategy for Cryptocurrency. The **Green/Red Candle Strategy** is a beginner-friendly, price action-based approach designed for trading high-liquidity crypto pairs like BTC/USDT, ETH/USDT, and other altcoins. With trading times tailored to your local time zone, you can execute trades during high-volatility sessions with ease. **What Is the Green/Red Candle Strategy?**. The Green/Red Candle Strategy uses the color of candlesticks on a 1-hour (H1) or 15-minute (M15) chart to determine whether to buy or sell. Traders select three daily trading times based on their local time zone, aligning with active market sessions (Asian, European, and U.S.) to maximize opportunities. **How to Trade with the Green/Red Candle Strategy**. * **Pick Trading Times**: Choose three daily times in your local time zone that align with global market activity. At each chosen trading time, check the most recently closed H1 or M15 candle:. * In Sydney, Australia (AEST, UTC+10), at 14:00 local time, if the H1 candle for BT...

evidences.title: candle close | CPA_008ONiCHaN on Binance Square
evidences.content: # candle close. The key to avoiding this mistake is simple, but difficult to apply when emotion and impatience take over: wait for the candle to close. 📌 Why is the candle close so important? Until a candle closes, its information is not definitive. If you wait for the candle to close, you will avoid being a victim of such deceptions. Wait for the candle close in the timeframe you are trading before confirming an entry. Here it is essential to wait for the closes of the 4H or even daily candles to confirm trends and avoid false signals. Often, the market may seem bearish halfway through the day, but recover by the end and close with a bullish candle. If the price breaks a resistance or support before the candle closes, it is not a real confirmation. Waiting for the candle close is one of the simplest yet most difficult rules to follow in trading. ✅ Observe the candle close in the timeframe you are trading. evidences.title: In Binance, a candle refers to a visual representation of | Younes wadoodi on Binance Square
evidences.content: # In Binance, a candle refers to a visual representation of price movements in the form of a candlestick chart. This is one of the most popular tools used in technical analysis to understand market trends and predict future price movements. Represents the opening and closing prices within a specific time period. A green (or white) candle indicates that the closing price is higher than the opening price (bullish). A red (or black) candle indicates that the closing price is lower than the opening price (bearish). The upper wick shows the highest price during the time period. The lower wick shows the lowest price during the time period. The open price is where the price starts during the selected time frame. The close price is where the price ends during the same period. High is the maximum price reached during the period. Low is the minimum price. 1 minute (1m): Shows price changes within one minute. 2. Hammer: Bullish reversal signal. 3. Shooting Star: Bearish reversal signal. 4. Engulfing: Strong trend reversal signal. Bullish Engulfing: Green candle fully engulfs the previous red candle. Bearish Engulfing: Red candle fully engulfs the previous green candle. 1. Open the Trading...

evidences.title: Bitcoin's Second Green Month Candle Signals Buy In a
evidences.content: Bitcoin's Second Green Month Candle Signals Buy In a | koinmilyoner on Binance Square. 12:00 PM・Nov 3, 2024 edited. Bitcoin's Second Green Month Candle Signals Buy. In a TradingView article, TradingShot advised buying when Bitcoin closes two consecutive green monthly candles. He argued that the multi-year chart reveals that the market rallies whenever Bitcoin records two consecutive green candles. Crypto analyst: Bitcoin price has three consecutive green candles and a definite accumulating period in 2021 bull run. During the 2017 bull run, Bitcoin had several straight green candles. BTC had three consecutive monthly green candles between January and March before entering an accumulation phase, resembling the 2021 bull run pattern. TradingShot noted that Bitcoin may post a third consecutive monthly green candle in November, making this a strong purchasing opportunity. Another reason Bitcoin may have a monthly green candle in November is that it has had positive monthly returns most of October. evidences.content: # With the closing of the 12-hour and daily candle of BTC dominance, there are two pieces of news for altcoins. **With the closing of the 12-hour and daily candle of BTC dominance, there are two pieces of news for altcoins.**. Let's start with the good news. The last 12-hour candle did indeed close red. Although altcoins probably did not feel this much. Without them, the recent #BTC fluctuations are hard to look at without tears. AT THE SAME TIME, the new 12-hour candle has a marker of potential highs. If we look at October 12-12, November 5, and December 2-3, such markers were reversal points and ensured a drop in BTC dominance. Especially the October and November ones. Also, a plus for altcoins is that the resistance level of 59.88% has held so far. **Bad news - the dominance of #BTC has transitioned into a sustainable uptrend on the daily timeframe for the first time since November 18. With key targets of 59.94%, 60.23%, 60.53%. And as we remember - trends are more important than markers. Especially when the trend is also on a higher timeframe. evidences.title: What time does the bitcoin market close? The day begins at | Zachmoon on Binance Square
evidences.content: Title: What time does the bitcoin market close? The day begins at | Zachmoon on Binance Square # What time does the bitcoin market close? The day begins at midnight 00:00:00 and ends at 23:59:59 UTC, if the question is asked at the close of the “daily” candle. What is a “Daily Close” in Cryptocurrency Trading? Although trading takes place throughout the hours of the day, the way traders measure cryptocurrency performance is through a daily 24-hour time frame, this is the “daily close.” At the end of a full day of trading (12:00 UTC), traders compare the price of a cryptocurrency with its price exactly 24 hours earlier. What are the peak hours for Bitcoin trading? Market Time Conversion Peak trading hours for the market are generally 8am to 4pm local time. These are the trading hours when trading volume in each region is usually the strongest. How much is 1 bitcoin worth? What time of day are cryptocurrency prices lowest? What is the best time to buy Bitcoin? What is the difference between daily and weekly candle? A daily candle is closed and a new candle is opened on the first trade after 00:00:00 UTC, or at 00:00:14 if no trade is completed within 14 seconds. The weekly candle...
